summary: XLSX workbook-view readback regressions exposed cross-carrier meta contamination (an ODS-produced sidecar could poison an XLSX workflow) and established the Intent-vs-Resolution persistence-boundary rule: persist declarative intent, discard computed resolution, recompute resolution from current data and pipeline state.

Commit anchors: e939623 (recover workbook_view from XLSX meta blob), b002fd6 (strip empty XLSX meta carrier markers), cae02d7 (root-cause analysis + canonical-meta-boundary assessment), d816d9a (prune resolution facets in persistence_boundary.py with carrier-boundary guards). summary: The xfail on BUG-UNRESOLVED-HELPER-VALUE-NAN-SEMANTICS-P4A was accepted as an executable sentinel for an unresolved FK-helper semantic contract: the framework has no defined shape for an unresolved helper value on the no-cleanup reimport path.
notes: Test suite is healthy with one expected failure. This record captures the concrete accepted NaN xfail as FK-helper contract evidence without generalizing one occurrence into a new xfail-extraction heuristic. The sentinel (test_missing_fk_key_helper_representation_without_cleanup in tests/roundtrip/test_pathological_cell_contracts.py) holds the open question: what canonical representation should an unresolved helper value carry after reimport without cleanup? A future extractor remains plausible if expected failures multiply, become release-relevant, or repeatedly confuse health/context interpretation.

notes: Four documents form this wave: semantic_boundary_review (YELLOW→GREEN, model boundary localization), functional_semantic_boundary_assessment (YELLOW, second opinion showing consumer evidence pointed at projection ergonomics rather than boundary work), assess_canonical_meta_boundary with Intent-vs-Resolution addendum (E2-narrow repair + framing breakthrough), and meta_flattening_hypothesis (orientation artifact, not a classic review). This wave is the analytical precursor to SIG-ACT-FUNCTIONAL-SEMANTIC-MODEL-SPLIT and SIG-ACT-CROSS-CARRIER-META-BOUNDARY. Commit anchors: d067675 (semantic boundary review creation), 93e476d (functional/semantic independent assessment), 8f1cba6 (canonical meta boundary assessment), d816d9a (Intent-vs-Resolution addendum + boundary fix), 6037f9f (meta flattening hypothesis).

summary: The meta-flattening hypothesis established the directional pivot: treat _meta as a carrier rather than a central ontology, own feature semantics in feature-local contracts, and use FK Helpers as the first falsification pilot before any cleanup.
notes: This orientation artifact (not a classic review) records the working hypothesis reached during post-domain-cleanup analysis: the problem is not _meta itself but the ontologization of _meta. Key direction shift: default risk balance changes from 'keep legacy metadata unless removal is proven safe' to 'simplify unless current code, tests, or consumers prove the layer is still needed.' The document is the direct intellectual precursor to the FK-helper functional model concept (commit d4a63f6) and the subsequent FK-helper consolidation arc (SIG-ACT-FK-HELPER-CONSOLIDATION-CLOSURE).

summary: _meta should be a carrier, not a central ontology; feature semantics should be owned by feature-local contracts — the meta-flattening direction that drove the FK-helper consolidation approach.
notes: Foundational directional finding that reframed the entire FK-helper meta work. Applied successfully in the consolidation arc; recorded as historical evidence for CONC-DOMAIN-META-SEMANTICS.

summary: Strategic assessment: the growing meta complexity risked becoming a dead end — increasing token cost, dependence on frontier models, loss of maintainability oversight — regardless of the project's continued feature delivery capacity.
notes: This assessment preceded and motivated the entire meta investigation arc (model boundary reviews, meta flattening hypothesis, FK-helper feature-local contracts). Concrete signals: SIG-REVIEW-002 had shifted attention toward 'semantic friction and repayable localized debt'; SIG-ACT-REIMPORT-HELPER-LEAKAGE-META-FRICTION documented that the reimport helper leakage fix carried high reasoning/token cost due to meta lifecycle disorganization; FK helper had resisted trivial refactoring across multiple architecture migration waves — attempts to clean it up pulled in too many entangled layers. The concern was not about the project failing to deliver features: it could still deliver with enough tokens. The concern was about the price — increasingly entangled meta, growing token consumption per session, mounting dependence on frontier models for context orientation, and the maintainer's own loss of traceability into why specific structures exist. The assessed trajectory: this could close off paths to simpler, leaner operation and make the project permanently dependent on ever-larger context windows. Hard to anchor in a single commit; see SIG-REVIEW-002 and SIG-ACT-REIMPORT-HELPER-LEAKAGE-META-FRICTION as the closest existing event anchors.

summary: Project Memory was conceived as automation preparation for the meta-investigation step sequence; after experiencing it, scope was corrected: PM itself already reduces enough of the immediate meta problem to defer further automation until real experience with this state shows recurring friction; LangGraph-style orchestration is not the current direction.
notes: The step sequence (warm storage → functional model → semantic model → code) emerged from the meta complexity investigation (SIG-ACT-META-COMPLEXITY-DEAD-END-RISK). It is a heavyweight process but produces durable, navigable reasoning trails. Project Memory was originally designed as the data substrate for automating this process — making concern extraction, review linkage, and orientation computable with less human effort per session. After building the initial governance cycle (SIG-ACT-PROJECT-MEMORY-GOVERNANCE-CYCLE) and experiencing it in practice, the assessment changed: the explicit, curated reasoning trail in project_memory already reduces session-entry token cost and reduces the maintainer reasoning monopoly without requiring automation. The benefit is real without orchestration. Corrected posture: build experience with the current lightweight governance state before adding automation layers. LangGraph (or any graph-based orchestration layer over the review/concern model) is not the current direction — not because it is wrong in principle, but because the curated model must be validated as stable and sufficient before orchestration overhead is added. Further automation only when recurring pain points make the cost of automation clearly lower than the cost of the manual governance process.

summary: Documentation-blind domain audit and FK-helper cleanup working context archived.
notes: The audit produced a transformation inventory, coupling map, and refactoring candidates. It supported the FK-helper/domain-cleanup/meta-simplification working step and shifted interpretation from possible architectural failure to structurally recoverable domain boundaries with historical feature sediment. The archived artifacts are historical snapshots, not maintained current architecture truth. Archived under docs/cold_storage/working_context/domain_cleanup_fk_helper_2026_06/.

summary: Repository-wide transformation/capability family inventory archived as the canonical June-2026 reference for implemented families and Functional Model coverage status.
notes: Cold-storage inventory path: docs/cold_storage/working_context/transformation_family_inventory_2026_06/transformation_family_inventory.adoc. The inventory was produced after the FK-helper/domain-cleanup analysis work to make implemented capability families discoverable for future Functional Model promotion. Project Memory records only this navigation and intent anchor; it does not duplicate the family table, coverage matrix, or implementation evidence. The inventory is not a roadmap, not a backlog, and not a second source of truth. Any follow-up work should be represented through normal FTR/backlog mechanisms.

summary: ADR accepted a contract-first domain/meta reset around a Functional-Model-derived bridge DSL, explicitly favoring bounded disruption over preserving historical implementation sediment.
notes: Genesis: a June-2026 requirement inquiry rebuilt the Functional Model from user intent, then the transformation family inventory and consumer checks showed that the implemented capability/meta surface is significantly broader than current Functional Model needs. Strategy options are preserved under docs/cold_storage/working_context/functional_model_inquiry/domain_reformation_strategy_inquiry.adoc and docs/cold_storage/working_context/functional_model_inquiry/refactoring_strategies/. The accepted rationale is intentionally counter-conservative: low external adoption makes breaking domain/meta changes acceptable; adapters, parsers, renderers, CLI, and useful fixtures remain assets; consumers are coverage checks, not golden masters; the bridge DSL grows slice by slice from Functional Model use cases; targeted deletion is allowed when obvious and low risk.

summary: XRef reformation exposed a broader Functional Model gap: spreadsheet roundtrip currently lacks an explicit projection/writeback-scope and entity-lifecycle concept for deleted/renamed axis entities, dangling relations, FK-helper references, schema maintenance changes, and possible future key-structure changes.
notes: During review of the XRef-only overhead audit, human review identified that resolved axis members are not only runtime resolution. They may be needed to know what the user saw and could edit during a workbook roundtrip. This matters when users delete matrix rows/columns, delete or rename source entities, or clean up schema/entity tables while relation rows still reference old values. Runtime resolution such as XRef column_keys or dense_axes.resolved may be legitimate scope evidence rather than mere sediment, but must not be promoted to durable canonical truth without an explicit policy. The current Functional Model does not yet define whether recomposition should preserve, drop, warn, hard-fail, or explicitly cascade such changes. The topic should be handled as a follow-up Functional Model concept/slice rather than absorbed into the current XRef contract.

summary: Repeated Functional/Semantic Model concept drift led to the first transformation-centered domain-contract registry slice, applying the earlier lesson that architecture boundaries stabilized only when violations became difficult.
notes: Experimental strategy pivot, not a proven replacement for Functional Model prose. The recurring problem was that prose corrections could dissolve under later AI-assisted documentation, semantic model edits, or implementation work, reintroducing adjacent abstractions, untraced rules, or divergent concepts. This slice adds flat structured JSON, generated extracts, and spreadsheet roundtrip support so future drift can become visible and eventually guardable through later checks or fitness functions; no such runtime guards are claimed by this event.
